name: Memorial Day
description: U.S. holiday honoring fallen service members — Decoration Day, poppies, and Arlington.

questions.q: On what day is U.S. Memorial Day observed?

source.notes: Set by the Uniform Monday Holiday Act, effective 1971.

questions.q: What was Memorial Day originally called?

source.notes: Renamed to Memorial Day; officially federal in 1967, holiday since 1971.

questions.q: Memorial Day was originally created to honor soldiers who died in which war?

source.notes: Began in 1868 by the Grand Army of the Republic (Union veterans).

questions.q: What flower is traditionally worn on Memorial Day in remembrance of fallen soldiers?

source.notes: Inspired by John McCrae's WWI poem 'In Flanders Fields.'

questions.q: Which 1915 WWI poem inspired the poppy tradition?
choices: The Charge of the Light Brigade

source.notes: Canadian Lt-Col McCrae, 1915. 'In Flanders fields the poppies blow / Between the crosses, row on row.'

questions.q: Which town is officially recognized as the 'birthplace of Memorial Day'?

source.notes: Designated by President Lyndon B. Johnson in 1966.

questions.q: Where is the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier?

source.notes: Guarded 24/7/365 since 1937 by Tomb Sentinels.

questions.q: Whose plantation was Arlington National Cemetery built on?

source.notes: Confiscated during the Civil War; the first burial was in 1864.

questions.q: Which famous race is held on Memorial Day weekend each year?

source.notes: Held the Sunday before Memorial Day since 1911.

questions.q: What bugle call is traditionally played at military funerals?

source.notes: 24 notes; composed during the Civil War in 1862.

questions.q: What time of day is the National Moment of Remembrance observed on Memorial Day?

source.notes: Established by the National Moment of Remembrance Act, 2000.

questions.q: Memorial Day is widely considered the 'unofficial start' of which season?

source.notes: Labor Day (first Monday of September) is the unofficial end.

questions.q: Which woman, known as the 'Angel of the Battlefield', founded the American Red Cross in 1881?

source.notes: Civil War nurse turned humanitarian.

questions.q: How does the U.S. flag fly on Memorial Day?

source.notes: Custom: half-staff dawn-noon (mourning), then full-staff (remembrance).

questions.q: Which Union general's General Order No. 11 in 1868 first proclaimed Decoration Day?

source.notes: Commander-in-Chief of the Grand Army of the Republic.

questions.q: Which year did Memorial Day become a federal holiday?

source.notes: Became official in 1971 via Uniform Monday Holiday Act.

questions.q: Which UK observance is similar to Memorial Day, held on the Sunday closest to November 11?

source.notes: British wear poppy pins for the season; observe 2 minutes silence at 11am.

questions.q: Which U.S. holiday — November 11 — is distinct from Memorial Day, honoring all military veterans (living)?

source.notes: Memorial Day = those who DIED in service; Veterans Day = ALL who served.

questions.q: How many U.S. service members have been killed in action across all wars?

source.notes: Approximately 1.3 million KIA across all U.S. wars; Civil War alone is ~620,000.

questions.q: Which president signed the National Moment of Remembrance Act in 2000?

source.notes: Signed December 28, 2000.
